modifications.rationale: The dataset's anatomical images have a voxel size of 1.96x1.96x5.0 mm. Using a resolution closer to the native voxel size (1.96mm) can improve registration accuracy by preserving more detailed structural information.

The functional data has a relatively thick slice thickness (5mm) compared to the in-plane resolution (1.96mm). Increasing the degrees of freedom from 6 to 12 allows for more flexible alignment, which can better account for the anisotropic voxels and improve coregistration accuracy.
The dataset includes both structural and functional data, and using a template space is more appropriate for multi-subject studies to ensure consistent registration across subjects.
The dataset lacks fieldmaps and has varying voxel sizes, making FSL-FAST less reliable. Template-based segmentation is more robust for such cases.
